name: obsidian-claude-accenture
description: Build and maintain a personal knowledge base from inside Claude. Drop files into a folder; Claude organizes them into searchable, linked notes. Compatible with Obsidian.
long_description:
Turn one or more folders into living knowledge bases that Claude builds with you in chat. Drop in PDFs, decks, transcripts, screenshots, or notes — Claude reads them, extracts the key ideas, and files them as structured wiki pages with cross-links. Save conversations as notes, ask the wiki questions in plain English, and let Claude run autonomous research loops that file their own findings.

Each knowledge folder is also a valid Obsidian vault, so you can open it in Obsidian for visual navigation, but Obsidian is not required. Optional features: auto-organize new files the moment you drop them in, and auto-snapshot every change to git so you can roll back.

Packaged for Accenture sideloading. Based on the LLM Wiki pattern by Andrej Karpathy.

tools.name: vault_scaffold
tools.description: Bootstrap an Obsidian vault: copy WIKI.md, _templates/, obsidian/, create wiki/ folders

tools.name: vault_read
tools.description: Read a file from a configured vault

tools.name: vault_write
tools.description: Write or overwrite a file in a configured vault. Auto-commits if the vault is a git repo.

tools.name: vault_list
tools.description: List files/folders in a vault path

tools.name: vault_search
tools.description: Search vault content (filename + body) with optional glob

tools.name: vault_active
tools.description: Show or set the active vault for this session

tools.name: wiki_ingest
tools.description: Read a source file from .raw/ and return its content for the model to extract entities and write wiki pages

tools.name: wiki_query
tools.description: Search wiki pages and return matched snippets for synthesis

tools.name: wiki_lint
tools.description: Return vault structure, link graph, and orphan candidates

tools.name: canvas_create
tools.description: Create a new Obsidian canvas (.canvas) file

tools.name: canvas_add_node
tools.description: Add a text/file/link node to a canvas

tools.name: canvas_list
tools.description: List canvases in the vault with node counts

tools.name: git_commit
tools.description: Stage and commit changes in the vault if it is a git repo

prompts.name: wiki
prompts.description: Bootstrap or check an Obsidian wiki vault
prompts.text: Bootstrap or check an Obsidian wiki vault

prompts.name: save
prompts.description: Save the current conversation as a structured wiki note
prompts.text: Save the current conversation as a structured wiki note

prompts.name: autoresearch
prompts.description: Autonomous research loop: search, synthesize, file into wiki
prompts.text: Autonomous research loop: search, synthesize, file into wiki

prompts.name: hot-update
prompts.description: Update wiki/hot.md with a session summary (replaces upstream Stop hook)
prompts.text: Update wiki/hot.md with a session summary

prompts.name: vault-use
prompts.description: Switch the active vault for this session
prompts.text: Switch the active vault for this session

vaults.title: Knowledge folders
vaults.description: Pick one or more folders to use as knowledge bases — one per project works well. Whenever you add, change, or remove a file in one of these folders, Claude will automatically update the knowledge base (the `wiki/` subfolder inside) with structured, linked notes. Empty folders are scaffolded for you on first use. Each folder also opens cleanly in Obsidian if you want a visual editor.

auto_commit.title: Auto-snapshot changes (git)
auto_commit.description: If a knowledge folder is a git repo, Claude saves a snapshot every time it writes a note. Lets you see history and roll back. Safe to leave on.

auto_watch.title: Auto watch
auto_watch.description: When you add, edit, or delete a file in a knowledge folder, Claude immediately reads it and updates the wiki — no need to ask.
